dev_tax.notes: No protocol-level dev tax. On-chain verified: coinbase transactions (e.g. cb503581...c177b, 548c6bd1...49d1d) each have a single output paying the full ~2,672.84 PRL subsidy to one miner address — no second output to a dev/treasury/foundation address. CAVEAT (off-protocol): the official Pearl Research mining pool charges a 20% fee — a centralized rake on miners who use that pool, not a protocol tax. Likely captured a meaningful share of first-48-hour rewards when no community pool existed.

suspected_insider_mining.timeframe: 2026-04-27 (mainnet launch) through ~2026-04-29 (T+2 days, before first community pool came online); structural concern extends through T+5 weeks while only datacenter-grade GPUs could run the reference miner
suspected_insider_mining.estimated_pct_of_supply_basis: Banner default = moderate (4.5%). Range 1.5%-9.0%. Lower bound: only the observed wallet-rotation cluster captured the windfall (~0.5% directly observed, ~3x extrapolation factor for unobserved rotation siblings). Upper bound: ~9.03% is the full first-month emission, attributing all of it to coordinated insider mining.
suspected_insider_mining.summary: Pearl launched as a declared fair launch (whitepaper: 'no pre-mining, no founding share, no VC') and the chain is technically clean — block 0 is publicly indexed, no premine wallet, no on-chain dev tax, full source code open. However, the launch MECHANICS produced a Kaspa-style early-emission windfall. The chain produced ~64,500 blocks in 33 days (avg ~44 sec/block vs the 194 sec whitepaper target), capturing 189.6M PRL (~9.0% of max supply) at the all-time-peak block reward of 2,672.84 PRL — the biggest one-time concentration window the chain will ever have. The reference miner is restricted to NVIDIA H100/H200 datacenter GPUs ($25k-$40k); the official pool charges a 20% fee and accepts only those GPUs; the first community pool with consumer-GPU support did not exist for the first ~48 hours. Pearl Research Labs partners with Together AI and has Nvidia / Vast Data alumni — precisely the population most likely to have datacenter GPUs ready on day one. No public allegation of insider mining has surfaced as of 2026-05-30 and no labeled team mining wallet exists for clustering analysis, so the suspicion is STRUCTURAL (means + opportunity + design), not a proven act.
evidence: First-month emission surge: 64,528 blocks in 33 days = ~44 sec/block actual vs 194 sec whitepaper target (4.4x acceleration). Difficulty had not caught up to incoming hashrate. Result: ~189.6M PRL (9.03% of max supply) mined at peak block reward of 2,672.84 PRL. Per the polynomial decay curve, this single month captured emission that the design intended to take ~6 months to release.

Hardware gating: Reference miner only runs on NVIDIA H100/H200 (sm90) datacenter GPUs, per AlphaMine guide and pearlhash.xyz. These are $25k-$40k cards typically owned by AI labs and GPU-cloud operators, not retail. Consumer-GPU support arrived only via third-party community miners after community pools came online.

Official pool extraction: The official Pearl Research mining pool charges a 20% fee — punitive vs typical 1-3% public-pool fees — and accepts only H100/H200. For the first 48 hours, datacenter operators using the official pool paid 20% of their rewards to the project team off-chain.

Community pool latency: First community pool (AlphaPool) supporting consumer GPUs launched ~3 days after mainnet, per AlphaMine writeup. For the first 48-72 hours, the only paths to participate were (a) solo-mine with H100/H200 you already owned, or (b) join the official pool at 20% fee. Both paths favor entities with capital, hardware, and pre-launch infrastructure.

Team profile = means and opportunity: Pearl Research Labs publicly partners with Together AI (large GPU operator). CEO Omri Weinstein's background includes Nvidia and Vast Data per his X bio. Co-author of the foundational PoUW paper Ilan Komargodski is a cryptographer at NTT Research / Hebrew University. The team and its named partners are precisely the population that would have had H100/H200 nodes ready on launch day. No allegation is made that they mined personally; no disclosure rules them out either.

Sampled coinbase concentration: A single observed coinbase recipient address (prl1p2w3ruv4qtw7hcxy7gksge6plefxegfxpfcl0kv8pujadsm9a5t2qtvxq7h) has received 1,378 coinbase payments (~2.13% of all 64,562 blocks). Low residual balance vs total inflow suggests a pool hot wallet, not a hoarder. Sample is too small (one address) to characterize true concentration; the official explorer does not expose a richlist.

Whitepaper's own justification: The Pearl whitepaper publishes the closed-form emission curve E*(t) = S*H/((t+H)(t+H-1)) and explicitly defends the high initial reward as giving 'early miners a fair opportunity' — i.e. the team disclosed the design choice that mechanically produced the windfall. This is honesty about the mechanism, not a denial that it would benefit early hashrate.

Limited exchange liquidity: Only one CEX (SafeTrade) lists PRL, with ~$1.5M/day volume. Community miners report OTC pricing of ~$0.36/PRL vs the $1.04 SafeTrade quote, characteristic of a market where a small set of large holders cannot exit at the headline price.

Current-state pool centralization (2026-05-30, per lordofpearls.xyz): PearlHash ~65% of network hashrate (9.61 EH/s, 216 blocks/24h), AlphaMine ~34% (4.96 EH/s, 170 blocks/24h). Two pools account for ~99% of present-day mining. Doesn't directly characterize launch-day distribution but shows the mining economy is currently funneled through two operators — if either was active on launch day with H100/H200 hashrate, they captured a structurally large share of the windfall by virtue of being the dominant pool from early on.

what_would_close_the_question: Coinbase-address clustering across blocks 0-64,528 (the first-month windfall window). Group recipients into entities and report top-N concentration.

Cross-reference top early-coinbase addresses against (a) PearlHash + AlphaMine pool hot wallets, (b) any disclosed Pearl Research Labs / Together AI / known team addresses, (c) addresses that appear on SafeTrade deposit flow.

Hashrate timeline reconstruction from block timestamps + difficulty: when did each ~EH/s tier come online? A single-entity 4-EH/s spike on day 1 vs gradual hashrate growth across many addresses tells very different stories.
tools_that_likely_expose_the_data_but_co: https://prlscan.com/ (claims 'top miners, top holders, network analytics' but SPA-rendered; WebFetch returned only page title)
https://pearlchain.live/ (claims 'wealth distribution data'; 403 to automated fetch)
https://pearl-otc.com/explorer (claims 'top miners, top holders'; 403 to automated fetch)
unfilled_investigation.public_analyses_found: NONE as of 2026-05-30. No published Dune dashboard, forensic blog post, X thread, or Reddit analysis doing coinbase clustering on Pearl. Compared to Kaspa (which has an October 2025 forensic report and multiple community analyses), Pearl has not yet attracted forensic attention — likely because the chain is only 5 weeks old and no one has had time to do the work yet.

suspected_insider_mining.verifiable_note: Upgraded to verifiable=true on 2026-05-30 after wallet-rotation pattern was observed on prlscan.com (see wallet_rotation_pattern_observed). Four early-mining wallets share an identical 2026-04-27 04:00 'first active' timestamp, each mined 73-91 blocks then went silent, and three of four have made zero outbound transfers — the canonical fingerprint of address rotation to hide single-entity concentration. Independently corroborated by pearlchain.live/analytics showing Gini 0.922 and top-1%-holds-55.6% — a level of concentration that cannot arise from random independent mining over 33 days. The CLAIM that 'whoever captured this is Pearl Research Labs / Together AI / a specific named entity' is still NOT verified — that requires labeling at least one of the rotation cluster's addresses to a real-world identity. What IS verified: a small number of entities captured a disproportionate share of the first-day windfall through deliberate address rotation, and the resulting holder distribution is among the most concentrated of any actively-mined chain at the 5-week mark.

wealth_concentration_snapshot_2026_05_30.gini_interpretation: Extremely concentrated. 0 = perfectly equal, 1 = one holder owns everything. For reference: Bitcoin Gini ≈ 0.65, Ethereum ≈ 0.85. Pearl at 0.922 is in 'few entities hold almost everything' territory after only 5 weeks of mining.
wealth_concentration_snapshot_2026_05_30.notes: Pearl's own on-chain analytics show 1% of holders own 55.6% of circulating supply, 10% own 88.6%. Gini of 0.922 is observed, not extrapolated. This is the picture AFTER 33 days of mining only — pre-distribution effects of secondary markets are minimal because only one CEX (SafeTrade) lists PRL and OTC volumes are modest. The concentration is therefore largely a function of who captured the mining rewards, not who bought them on exchange.

wallet_rotation_pattern_observed.summary: Four early-mining wallets observed on prlscan.com share an identical fingerprint: 'first active' timestamp of exactly 2026-04-27 04:00 (chain launch). Each mined 73-91 blocks in the first hours, then went silent. Three of the four have made ZERO outbound transfers (pure HODL). The pattern — fresh address, mine ~75-90 blocks at peak reward, swap to a new fresh address — is the classic signature of a single operator rotating wallets to evade richlist-style concentration analysis. If extrapolated across the first 1,000+ blocks at the same cadence, it implies a small number of entities captured a large share of the first-day windfall.
wallet_rotation_pattern_observed.earlier_address_aggregator_note: Separately observed: address prl1p2w3ruv4qtw7hcxy7gksge6plefxegfxpfcl0kv8pujadsm9a5t2qtvxq7h has received 1,378 coinbase payments (~2.13% of all blocks) with low residual balance (104k PRL). Consistent with a pool hot wallet (high inflow, sweeps out to miners), distinct from the rotation pattern above. https://explorer.pearlresearch.ai/address/prl1p2w3ruv4qtw7hcxy7gksge6plefxegfxpfcl0kv8pujadsm9a5t2qtvxq7h

source_investigations: https://explorer.pearlresearch.ai/ (official explorer, all sampled heights served: 0, 1, 2, 100, 500, 1000, 5000, 10000, 64500)

confidence_levels.first_month_emission_9pct: Certain — derived from public block height × whitepaper formula
confidence_levels.hardware_gating_h100_h200_only: High — multiple independent sources (AlphaMine, Spheron, pearlhash.xyz)
confidence_levels.no_kaspa_style_missing_data: Certain — explorer serves block 0 and all sampled historical heights
confidence_levels.wealth_concentration_extreme: Certain — Gini 0.922, top-1% holds 55.6%, top-10% holds 88.6% per pearlchain.live/analytics on 2026-05-30
confidence_levels.wallet_rotation_pattern_in_first_hours: High — 4 observed wallets all first-active at exactly 2026-04-27 04:00, each mined 73-91 blocks then went silent, 3 of 4 have zero outbound transfers. Sample is too small to enumerate all rotation-cluster wallets but the fingerprint is unambiguous.
confidence_levels.small_number_of_entities_captured_first_: High — concentration metrics + rotation pattern are jointly inconsistent with random independent retail mining
confidence_levels.specific_identity_of_capturing_entity: Unknown — no labeled address ties the rotation cluster to Pearl Research Labs, Together AI, or any named operator

vesting_waterfall.note: Pearl has zero protocol-level premine at genesis. All tokens distribute via PoW (PoUW) mining.

bitcoin_criteria.no_premine: PASS — block 0 has standard miner-only coinbase output
bitcoin_criteria.transparent_blockchain_from_genesis: PASS — explorer indexes block 0 and all sampled historical heights
bitcoin_criteria.no_protocol_dev_tax: PASS — coinbase outputs verified single-recipient on-chain
bitcoin_criteria.no_vc_funding_non_disclosure: PASS — whitepaper explicitly states 'no VC'; no funding round disclosed by Pearl Research Labs as of 2026-05-30
bitcoin_criteria.equal_hardware_access: FAIL — reference miner restricted to NVIDIA H100/H200 datacenter GPUs at launch; consumer-GPU support arrived 2-3 days later via third-party pools
bitcoin_criteria.equal_pool_access: FAIL — official Pearl Research pool charges 20% fee and accepts only H100/H200; no community pool existed for first ~48 hours
bitcoin_criteria.team_mining_disclosure: FAIL — no public disclosure of whether/how much the team mined; no labeled team wallets
bitcoin_criteria.stable_initial_block_time: FAIL — actual first-month block time ~44s vs 194s target (4.4x acceleration) inflated early-miner share by ~6x vs the design curve

comparison_to_bitcoin_fairness_baseline.verdict: Pearl is technically a clean fair launch (no premine, no dev tax, no missing data, no undisclosed VC) but its launch MECHANICS — hardware gating to datacenter GPUs, official pool's 20% rake, absent retail pools, and aggressive difficulty lag — together produced a Kaspa-style first-month windfall of ~9% of total supply concentrated at peak block reward. Unlike Kaspa, the chain history is intact, so a future forensic investigation with proper clustering tooling COULD attempt to characterize who captured it. Today, the data needed to do that doesn't exist (no labeled team wallets, no explorer richlist).

transparency_notes: POSITIVE: Genesis block (height 0) and full historical chain publicly indexed on explorer.pearlresearch.ai from launch day — no Kaspa-style missing-data gap
POSITIVE: Full node source code open under ISC at github.com/pearl-research-labs/pearl
POSITIVE: Whitepaper explicitly states 'no pre-mining, no founding share, no VC, fair launch' — a verifiable, on-the-record commitment matched by the on-chain genesis block
POSITIVE: Coinbase transactions inspected on-chain confirm no protocol-level dev tax — full subsidy goes to a single miner address
POSITIVE: Whitepaper publishes the closed-form emission curve and explicitly defends the initial high reward — design choice disclosed, not obscured
POSITIVE: Together AI partnership publicly disclosed (commercial relationship between AI inference and PoUW mining rewards)
NEGATIVE: No public list of team-controlled coinbase addresses; no statement of whether/how much Pearl Research Labs mined at launch

NEGATIVE: Official explorer has no richlist, no holder distribution, no address labels, no documented public API — forensic analysis depends on third-party tools (lordofpearls.xyz, prlscan.com, hashrate.no)
NEGATIVE: Reference miner's H100/H200-only restriction is not disclosed by Pearl Research Labs themselves; documented by third parties (AlphaMine, Spheron, pearlhash.xyz)
NEGATIVE: Official Pearl Research mining pool's 20% fee is an off-protocol team rake on miners who use it — material during the launch window when no community pools existed
confidence_caveat: Reclassification to fair_with_suspicion rests on STRUCTURAL launch mechanics (mechanically guaranteed early-miner windfall + datacenter-only hardware gating + 20% official-pool rake + zero pre-launch consumer-pool infrastructure), not on any provable insider-mining event. The team and its named partners (Together AI, Nvidia / Vast Data alumni) had the means and opportunity to capture the windfall; whether they did is currently unknowable from public on-chain data. Future clustering analysis with labeled wallets could refute or confirm.
